summary: The diff implements REQ-9 and REQ-10 fully in the frontend (resumeAnalysis.ts and JobRolePrediction.tsx), and implements NLP preprocessing (stopword removal + lemmatization) in the frontend library. However, REQ-8 requires backend NLP preprocessing in backend/main.mo, but the modified main.mo shows no stopword removal or lemmatization logic — it appears to have been simplified/stripped rather than enhanced. The ATS scoring and preprocessing are handled client-side only.

missing_requirements.requirement: Enhance the backend ATS score computation to include NLP preprocessing steps (stopword removal and lemmatization) on the resume text before performing TF-IDF cosine similarity comparison against the job description for the selected role.
missing_requirements.reason: The modified backend/main.mo shows no ATS score computation function, no stopword removal, and no lemmatization logic. The file appears stripped down to only getJobRoles, getCertifications, getResumeTemplates, getResumeTemplate, getActionVerbSuggestions, and addResumeTemplate — no computeAtsScore or any NLP preprocessing functions are present. While NLP preprocessing was added client-side in resumeAnalysis.ts, the requirement explicitly targets the backend ATS score computation.

hallucinated_features.description: The backend/main.mo was simplified or stripped of existing backend logic such as ATS score computation, reducing backend functionality rather than enhancing it.
hallucinated_features.reason: The diff shows main.mo with only basic query functions and no ATS scoring or prediction logic, suggesting previously existing backend features may have been removed, which was not requested.
